Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple A basic-level cyber attack targeting healthcare institutions involves gaining initial access through phishing, executing malicious files via reg.exe utility, and causing impact by destroying data.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends phishing emails with malicious attachments or links to trick employees into downloading and executing them.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-635: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: Upon opening the attachment/link, the reg.exe utility executes the malicious file, allowing the attacker to run unauthorized commands.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker modifies critical files, leading to data destruction, which disrupts operations and services within the healthcare institution.

Scenario.summary: This scenario outlines a simple cyber attack chain executed by the FunnyDream campaign against healthcare organizations. Initial access is achieved through phishing, followed by execution of malicious files using reg.exe, culminating in data destruction impacting critical systems and services.
